expectations.text: Uses findings from the current repository and current feature-dev skill rather than generic advice.
expectations.evidence: The response cites skills/feature-dev/SKILL.md, feature-dev agent files, and neighboring repo skills such as research, create-plan, and implement-plan.

expectations.text: Asks targeted clarifying questions that are grounded in concrete risks or ambiguities.
expectations.evidence: Its questions concern the desired operating model, artifact creation, caution level in unfamiliar repos, question caps, and architecture-choice behavior.

expectations.text: Caps clarifying questions to a small, prioritized set rather than turning discovery into a broad interview.
expectations.evidence: The response asks eight separate questions across operating model, artifact behavior, caution level, unfamiliar-repo signals, and approval behavior. That is a broad interview rather than a tightly prioritized set.

expectations.text: Separates current understanding, open questions, and a recommended approach.
expectations.evidence: It uses distinct sections for what should change, questions to ask before editing, and the recommended approach before editing anything.

expectations.text: Explains why the proposed changes would help with unfamiliar repositories.
expectations.evidence: It explains that unfamiliar repos need repo triage, better risk-ranked questions, and a planning path before edits because missing constraints early is the main failure mode.

claims.claim: The repository already suggests a composition pattern using neighboring planning-oriented skills.
claims.evidence: The response ties the recommendation to create-plan, implement-plan, and research, which are present in this repo and plausibly support the claimed composition model.

uncertainties: It is still unclear whether feature-dev should be an end-to-end skill or a router into create-plan and implement-plan.
needs_review: Confirm what counts as product-sized work in this repo so scaling rules can be written precisely.
